paper_title: Earth System Model Analysis of How Astronomical Forcing Is Imprinted Onto the Marine Geological Record: The Role of the Inorganic (Carbonate) Carbon Cycle and Feedbacks

methods.description: The cGENIE model is configured to include spatiotemporal insolation forcing based on astronomical parameters, which are updated every 1,000 years to recalculate daily mean insolation at each latitude. This method is used to simulate past climate events and future carbon cycle responses.

steps.description: Combine a dynamic 3D ocean model with a simplified 2D energy-moisture-balance atmosphere model.
steps.searchable_summary: integrate 3D ocean model with 2D atmosphere model

steps.description: Include a detailed representation of ocean biogeochemical cycles and the long-term carbon cycle.
steps.searchable_summary: add biogeochemical and carbon cycle representation

steps.description: Update parameters for eccentricity, obliquity, and precession every 1,000 years using the La2004 astronomical solution.
Recalculate daily mean insolation at each latitude based on updated astronomical parameters.

methods.description: A series of experiments are conducted using the cGENIE model to investigate the effects of astronomical forcing on climate and the carbon cycle over 4 million years.

steps.description: Run cGENIE transiently for 4 million years to evaluate astronomical cycles with periods up to 500 kyr.
Conduct a set of five simulations for both SYMM and ASYM configurations, increasing the complexity of the carbon cycle sequentially.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct simulations with varying carbon cycle complexity

steps.description: In Exp.0, disable all carbon cycle feedbacks and fix radiative forcing with respect to atmospheric CO2 composition.
steps.searchable_summary: disable carbon cycle feedbacks and fix radiative forcing

steps.description: In Exp.1, allow variations in atmospheric CO2 to modulate surface temperature and fix export production of POC and CaCO3.
steps.searchable_summary: allow CO2 variations and fix export production

steps.description: In Exp.2, allow POC export to vary with light and nutrient availability, and remineralize through the water column.
